Copilot said: ## 🚀 Step-by-Step: Create EmpireBox Founders USB --- ##
🚀 Step-by-Step: Create EmpireBox Founders USB
📋 What You Need
Item	Details
USB Flash Drive	16GB minimum (32GB recommended)
Computer	Windows, Mac, or Linux
Internet	For downloads
Time	~30 min to create USB, ~45 min to install
STEP 1: Download Ubuntu Server 24.04
Go to: https://ubuntu.com/download/server
Click "Download Ubuntu Server 24.04.x LTS"
Save the .iso file (~2.5GB)
Wait for download to complete
STEP 2: Download Rufus (Windows) or Use Disk Utility (Mac)
Windows:
Go to: https://rufus.ie
Download Rufus Portable
No install needed - just run it
Mac:
Use built-in Disk Utility + Terminal
Or download balenaEtcher: https://etcher.balena.io
Linux:
bash
sudo apt install usb-creator-gtk
# Or use dd command
STEP 3: Create Bootable Ubuntu USB
Using Rufus (Windows):
Insert USB drive
Open Rufus
Configure:
Code
Device:           [Select your USB drive]
Boot selection:   [Click SELECT → choose ubuntu-24.04...iso]
Partition scheme: GPT
Target system:    UEFI
File system:      FAT32
Click START
If asked, choose "Write in ISO Image mode"
Wait ~5-10 minutes
DON'T REMOVE USB YET!
Using balenaEtcher (Mac/Linux):
Open Etcher
Select the Ubuntu ISO
Select your USB drive
Click Flash
Wait for completion
DON'T REMOVE USB YET!
STEP 4: Download EmpireBox Files
Option A: Download ZIP (Easiest)
Go to: https://github.com/r22gir/Empire
Click green "Code" button
Click "Download ZIP"
Extract the ZIP file
Find the folder: Empire-main/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/
Option B: Git Clone
bash
git clone https://github.com/r22gir/Empire.git
cd Empire/founders_usb_installer/
STEP 5: Copy EmpireBox to USB
Open your USB drive in File Explorer/Finder
You should see Ubuntu files already on it
Copy the entire empirebox folder to the USB root
Your USB should now look like:

Code
USB Drive (E:)
├── boot/
├── casper/
├── EFI/
├── ...other Ubuntu files...
│
└── empirebox/              ← YOU ADDED THIS
    ├── install-founders.sh
    ├── docker-compose.yml
    ├── config.json
    ├── scripts/
    │   └── ebox
    ├── openclaw/
    ├── products/
    ├── control/
    └── dashboard/
STEP 6: Safely Eject USB
Windows: Right-click USB → Eject
Mac: Drag to trash or click eject icon
Linux: sudo umount /dev/sdX
STEP 7: Boot Beelink from USB
Plug USB into Beelink EQR5
Connect monitor, keyboard, ethernet
Power on Beelink
Press DEL or F2 repeatedly to enter BIOS
In BIOS:
Go to Boot menu
Set USB as first boot device
Or use Boot Override to select USB
Save & Exit (F10)
Beelink will boot from USB
STEP 8: Install Ubuntu
When Ubuntu installer starts:

Select "Try or Install Ubuntu Server"
Choose Language → English
Choose Keyboard → Your layout
Network: Select your ethernet (should auto-detect)
Proxy: Leave blank (press Enter)
Mirror: Leave default (press Enter)
Storage:
Select "Use an entire disk"
Select the NVMe drive (500GB)
Confirm "Done"
Profile Setup:
Code
Your name:     empirebox
Server name:   empirebox-founders
Username:      empirebox
Password:      [YOUR SECURE PASSWORD]
SSH: Select "Install OpenSSH server" ✓
Featured Snaps: Skip (don't select any)
Install: Wait for installation (~10-15 min)
Reboot: Remove USB when prompted
STEP 9: First Boot - Login
Beelink will reboot into Ubuntu
Login:
Code
Username: empirebox
Password: [the password you set]
STEP 10: Mount USB and Run Installer
Plug the USB back in, then:

bash
# Find USB device
lsblk

# Create mount point
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/usb

# Mount USB (usually sda1 or sdb1)
sudo mount /dev/sda1 /mnt/usb

# Verify files are there
ls /mnt/usb/empirebox/

# Run the installer!
sudo bash /mnt/usb/empirebox/install-founders.sh
STEP 11: Wait for Installation (~30-45 min)
The script will:

Code
[✓] Update system packages
[✓] Install Docker
[✓] Generate secure credentials
[✓] Install Ollama
[✓] Pull AI models (llama3.1, codellama, nomic)  ← Takes longest
[✓] Start core services
[✓] Install ebox CLI
[✓] Configure firewall
Watch the terminal - it shows progress.

STEP 12: Done! Access Your Founders Unit
When you see:

Code
=== EmpireBox Founders Install Complete ===

  Dashboard:      http://192.168.x.x
  OpenClaw:       http://192.168.x.x:7878
  Control Center: http://192.168.x.x:8001
  Portainer:      http://192.168.x.x:9000

  Run 'ebox list' to see all products
You're done! 🎉

STEP 13: Test It!
bash
# List all products
ebox list

# Check status
ebox status

# Start all products
ebox bundle full

# View dashboard
# Open browser: http://<IP-ADDRESS>

messages.content:
I can see you have a well-organized system already in progress. Let me summarize the current state and provide recommendations for addressing the chat continuity issues:

## 📊 Current Repository State

### Open Issues
| Issue | Title | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| [#20](https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/issues/20) | MAIN CHAT HISTORY Management | Open - Central hub for context tracking |
| [#21](https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/issues/21) | Copilot Chat Session Summary (2026-02-19) | Open - Session summary |

### Open Pull Request
```list type="pr"
data:
- url: "https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/pull/33"
  repository: "r22gir/Empire"
  state: "open"
  draft: true
  title: "Add MAIN CHAT HISTORY archive summaries for Feb 16–18 sessions"
  number: 33
  created_at: "9 hours ago"
  author: "Copilot"
```

### What's Already in Place
- **`docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE/` folder** - For storing chat summaries
- **`chat back up text.txt`** - Main backup file
- **`wed_feb_18_2026_main_empirebox_chat.json`** - Chat export

---

## 🔧 Recommendations for Chat Continuity

### 1. **Merge PR #33 Now**
The draft PR adds 3 session summaries (Feb 16-18) to your archive. This is ready to merge and will give you searchable history.

### 2. **Standardized Chat Recovery Protocol**
When starting a new chat session (especially after model changes), paste this context block:

```markdown
## Empire Project Context
Repository: r22gir/Empire
Central Hub: https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/issues/20
Chat Archive: docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE/
Last Session: [date]
Current Task: [brief description]
```

### 3. **Create a "Context Resume" File**
I can help you create a `CONTEXT_RESUME.md` file that you can always reference when starting fresh:

```markdown
# Empire Project Quick Context

## What This Project Is
EmpireBox - A comprehensive business automation platform

## Key Files
- Task Plan: `docs_TASKS_AND_PROJECT_PLAN_Version7.md`
- Chat History: `docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE/`
- Main Backup: `chat back up text.txt`

## Recent Decisions
[Auto-updated from Issue #20 comments]

## Current Sprint
[Current active work items]
```

---

## 📋 Next Steps

Would you like me to:

1. **Help you merge PR #33** to archive the Feb 16-18 sessions?
2. **Create the CONTEXT_RESUME.md file** in your repo?
3. **Process the 2MB text file** you mentioned and add it to the archive?
4. **Add a comment to Issue #20** summarizing today's session?

Let me know which actions to prioritize, or if you'd like to upload/paste the new chat content that needs to be integrated!

data.body: Maintain an up-to-date record of all incoming chat data for the project. This issue is intended to ensure that the MAIN CHAT HISTORY is continuously updated to reflect new content, discussions, and decisions as they occur. Regularly review chat logs, aggregate relevant information, and synchronize it with this central history to support effective project tracking and team awareness.

data.body:
### Chat Session Summary (2026-02-19)

**What we did:**
- Verified new chat history preservation workflows
- Discussed frustrations of chat loss on new devices
- Linked Issue #20 as the central hub for context tracking
- Uploaded chat backups (text file, JSON) into the repository
- Created `docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE/` folder and README for future chat summary uploads
- Decided to keep `docs_TASKS_AND_PROJECT_PLAN_Version7.md` updated with a chat backup reminder task

**What's in the repo:**
- `chat back up text.txt` (**main backup file**)
- `wed_feb_18_2026_main_empirebox_chat.json` (chat export)
- All architecture, task plan, and implementation files verified

**What still needs to be done:**
- Add a recurring chat backup reminder to the tasks file
- Keep using Issue #20 after every significant chat or decision

**Links:**
- [MAIN CHAT HISTORY Management (Issue #20)](https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/issues/20)
- [CHAT_ARCHIVE folder](https://github.com/r22gir/Empire/tree/main/docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE)

*This comment was added to permanently log the preservation process. Future major sessions should be summarized in either Issue #20 or an archive file.*

data.body:

### 2. Open PR Ready to Merge

```

### 3. Drafted New Issue for Chat Continuity System

I've drafted a new issue to address the chat disruption problem:

````yaml type="draft-issue"
type: draft-issue
tag: "chat-backup-auto-context"
issueNumber: 0
repository: "r22gir/Empire"
state: "draft"
title: "Automate periodic chat backup and decision context unification"
description: |-
    ## Problem
    Recent operations have been disrupted by chat interruptions and switching between AI models, resulting in loss of context and inconsistent decisions.

    ## Goal
    Design and implement a system to periodically record and backup chat content (including Copilot/AI chat history and decision logs), then unify that data for consistent decision-making across software and AI agents. Aim to minimize disruptions and improve productivity.

    ## Requirements
    - Scheduled periodic backup of chat history (both user/AI messages)
    - Ability to import external chat text files for historical context
    - Central mechanism to unify and synthesize decision context from multiple chat sessions
    - Provide interfaces (API/endpoints/UI) for retrieving unified context
    - Integration with productivity tools and decision logs
    - Optionally, automate backup to cloud or local storage
    - Solution must preserve privacy and sensitive data

    ## Suggestions
    - Use a background worker or scheduled job in EmpireBox for backups
    - Store chat logs in PostgreSQL with timestamps and source IDs
    - Develop an "unification" service to synthesize actionable summaries/contexts
    - Surface unified context via dashboard/API for all decision agents
    - Allow custom import/export for external chat records
    - Add logging for disruption events (e.g. model switch, assignment changes)

    ## Outcome
    - Unified, reliable context for consistent decisions
    - Minimized workflow disruptions
    - Productivity improvement for founder and agents

    ---
    This issue is a high-priority feature request following major team disruptions caused by model switches and chat context loss. Please suggest implementation strategies and begin planning integration into EmpireBox.
assignees: ["r22gir"]
labels: ["chat", "backup", "productivity", "automation"]
issueType: "feature request"
````

---

## 📝 Recommended Next Steps

1. **Merge PR #33** — This adds Feb 16-18 chat summaries to `docs/CHAT_ARCHIVE/`
2. **Create the drafted issue above** — To track chat backup automation
3. **Add a comment to Issue #20** summarizing today's session (Feb 20)
4. **Create `CONTEXT_RESUME.md`** — A quick-reference file for resuming context after model switches

## Crypto Payments Feature Proposal

### Overview
Enable customers and users to pay for EmpireBox products, services, subscriptions, and marketplace transactions using cryptocurrencies and stablecoins.

### Payment Options Discussed:
- **Solana Pay integration** (priority for Solana Seeker phone)
- **Simple USDC/USDT acceptance** (business payments)
- **Coinbase Commerce or Stripe Crypto** (where available)
- Potential support for hardware purchases, SaaS, marketplace

### Scope
- MarketF (Marketplace): Allow crypto checkout/escrow
- EmpireBox: Accept crypto for subscriptions and software
- ContractorForge/LuxeForge: Let clients pay invoices in USDC/crypto

### Next Steps
- Create a detailed `docs/CRYPTO_PAYMENTS_SPEC.md`
- Research Solana Pay Next.js integration
- Assess regulatory requirements (KYC, refund policy, conversion)
- Write demo/test suite for USDC acceptance

---

data.body:
## EmpireBox: Full Business Model, Hardware Hierarchy, Conservative Projections, and Launch CAC Study

### 1. Product & Hardware Hierarchy
- **EmpireBox (Core Platform)**: All product orchestration, automation, licensing. Brain is OpenClaw AI.
- **Hardware Prioritization**:
  - **Mini PC**: First-class hardware (local AI, best performance & multitasking). Designed for core users.
  - **Solana Seeker Phone**: Second, mobile/contractor MVP. Runs scan/list/EmpireAssist, but onboard AI limited by RAM; offloads to cloud when needed.
  - **Empire Tablet**: Larger screen, field ops, job sites, photo measurements; same features as phone with improved UX.
  - **Mobile App**: For basic/contractor features and remote scanning.
- Phone can operate standalone (MVP), but optimal experience when paired with Mini PC.
- All sub-products (MarketForge, ForgeCRM, SupportForge, MarketF, RelistApp, etc) plug in as modules.

### 2. Conservative Business Projections (Y3)
- **5% Scenario (very conservative):**
  - MarketF: $8.65M x 0.05 = $433K
  - LLCFactory: $2M x 0.05 = $100K
  - MarketForge: $1.9M x 0.05 = $95K
  - RelistApp: $1.5M x 0.05 = $75K
  - ApostApp: $1.5M x 0.05 = $75K
  - SocialForge: $800K x 0.05 = $40K

references.title: Automate periodic chat backup and decision context unification
references.description:
## Problem
Recent operations have been disrupted by chat interruptions and switching between AI models, resulting in loss of context and inconsistent decisions.

## Goal
Design and implement a system to periodically record and backup chat content (including Copilot/AI chat history and decision logs), then unify that data for consistent decision-making across software and AI agents. Aim to minimize disruptions and improve productivity.

## Requirements
- Scheduled periodic backup of chat history (both user/AI messages)
- Ability to import external chat text files for historical context
- Central mechanism to unify and synthesize decision context from multiple chat sessions
- Provide interfaces (API/endpoints/UI) for retrieving unified context
- Integration with productivity tools and decision logs
- Optionally, automate backup to cloud or local storage
- Solution must preserve privacy and sensitive data

## Suggestions
- Use a background worker or scheduled job in EmpireBox for backups
- Store chat logs in PostgreSQL with timestamps and source IDs
- Develop an "unification" service to synthesize actionable summaries/contexts
- Surface unified context via dashboard/API for all decision agents
- Allow custom import/export for external chat records
- Add logging for disruption events (e.g. model switch, assignment changes)

## Outcome
- Unified, reliable context for consistent decisions
- Minimized workflow disruptions
- Productivity improvement for founder and agents

---
This issue is a high-priority feature request following major team disruptions caused by model switches and chat context loss. Please suggest implementation strategies and begin planning integration into EmpireBox.

## Crypto Payments Feature Proposal

### Overview
Enable customers and users to pay for EmpireBox products, services, subscriptions, and marketplace transactions using cryptocurrencies and stablecoins.

### Payment Options Discussed:
- **Solana Pay integration** (priority for Solana Seeker phone)
- **Simple USDC/USDT acceptance** (business payments)
- **Coinbase Commerce or Stripe Crypto** (where available)
- Potential support for hardware purchases, SaaS, marketplace

### Scope
- MarketF (Marketplace): Allow crypto checkout/escrow
- EmpireBox: Accept crypto for subscriptions and software
- ContractorForge/LuxeForge: Let clients pay invoices in USDC/crypto

### Next Steps
- Create a detailed `docs/CRYPTO_PAYMENTS_SPEC.md`
- Research Solana Pay Next.js integration
- Assess regulatory requirements (KYC, refund policy, conversion)
- Write demo/test suite for USDC acceptance

data.body:
## EmpireBox: Full Business Model, Hardware Hierarchy, Conservative Projections, and Launch CAC Study

### 1. Product & Hardware Hierarchy
- **EmpireBox (Core Platform)**: All product orchestration, automation, licensing. Brain is OpenClaw AI.
- **Hardware Prioritization**:
  - **Mini PC**: First-class hardware (local AI, best performance & multitasking). Designed for core users.
  - **Solana Seeker Phone**: Second, mobile/contractor MVP. Runs scan/list/EmpireAssist, but onboard AI limited by RAM; offloads to cloud when needed.
  - **Empire Tablet**: Larger screen, field ops, job sites, photo measurements; same features as phone with improved UX.
  - **Mobile App**: For basic/contractor features and remote scanning.
- Phone can operate standalone (MVP), but optimal experience when paired with Mini PC.
- All sub-products (MarketForge, ForgeCRM, SupportForge, MarketF, RelistApp, etc) plug in as modules.

### 2. Conservative Business Projections (Y3)
- **5% Scenario (very conservative):**
  - MarketF: $8.65M x 0.05 = $433K
  - LLCFactory: $2M x 0.05 = $100K
  - MarketForge: $1.9M x 0.05 = $95K
  - RelistApp: $1.5M x 0.05 = $75K
  - ApostApp: $1.5M x 0.05 = $75K
  - SocialForge: $800K x 0.05 = $40K
  - Other: $850K x 0.05 = $43K
  - **Total:** ~$861K/year
- **10% Scenario (conservative):**
  - MarketF: $8.65M x 0.10 = $865K
  - LLCFactory: $2M x 0.10 = $200K
  - MarketForge: $1.9M x 0.10 = $190K
  - RelistApp: $1.5M x 0.10 = $150K
  - ApostApp: $1.5M x 0.10 = $150K
  - SocialForge: $800K x 0.10 = $80K
  - Other: $850K x 0.10 = $85K
  - **Total:** ~$1.72M/year

### 3. MarketForge Ad Cost Study for Launch
- **Estimated Ad Channels/CAC:**
  - Google Ads: $1.20–$3/click, CAC $60–150
  - FB/Meta: $0.80–$2.50/click, CAC $100–300
  - IG: $1.50–$4.50/click, CAC $150–400
  - TikTok: $1–$2.50/click, CAC $130–350
  - YouTube: $2–$5/click, CAC $200–500
  - Influencers: $500–$5000/post
- **Minimum viable launch budget:** $12K–$25K for 200–500 users
- **MarketForge** can integrate "Ad Boost/Reporting": Multi-channel spend, CAC, conversion metrics; dashboard for ROI.

### 4. Next Steps & Actions
- Build new Project Board for hierarchy, hardware, and launch budgeting
- Analyze organic vs. paid CAC, update MVP plan if ad conversion is below target
- Validate phone MVP (RAM, cloud AI fallback, on-device inference if possible)
- Complete docs for investor/founder understanding (this issue is the current master record)

_Last revised: 2026-02-19._

references.content:
1| # EmpireBox - Complete Platform for Resellers
2| 
3| ![EmpireBox](https://img.shields.io/badge/EmpireBox-Platform-blue)
4| ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Proprietary-red)
5| ![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Development-yellow)
6| 
7| A comprehensive system combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, agent safeguards, emergency stop protocols, mobile marketplace functionality, enterprise customer support, and economic intelligence.
8| 
9| ## 🎯 Ecosystem Overview
10| 
11| | Product | Description | Port |
12| |---------|-------------|------|
13| | 🛒 MarketForge | Multi-marketplace listing & inventory | 8010 |
14| | 🏗️ ContractorForge | Contractor business management | 8020 |
15| | ��� LuxeForge | Custom workroom / window treatments | 8030 |
16| | 💬 SupportForge | Customer support platform | 8040 |
17| | 📈 LeadForge | Lead generation & nurturing | 8050 |
18| | 📦 ShipForge | Shipping & label management | 8060 |
19| | 👥 ForgeCRM | Customer relationship management | 8070 |
20| | 🔄 RelistApp | Cross-platform relisting | 8080 |
21| | 📱 SocialForge | Social media management | 8090 |
22| | 🏢 LLCFactory | Business formation & compliance | 8100 |
23| | 📜 ApostApp | Document apostille services | 8110 |
24| | 🤖 EmpireAssist | AI assistant for all products | 8120 |
25| | 💰 EmpirePay | Crypto payments & EMPIRE token | 8130 |
26| 
27| ## 📁 Repository Structure
28|

1| # EmpireBox Backend API
2| 
3| FastAPI backend for EmpireBox - combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, and AI-powered marketplace automation.
4| 
5| ## Features
6| 
7| - **License Key System**: Generate, validate, and activate license keys for hardware bundles
8| - **Shipping Integration**: EasyPost integration for comparing rates and purchasing labels
9| - **Pre-order Management**: Handle hardware bundle pre-orders with Stripe payments
10| - **Authentication**: JWT-based auth with signup, login, and token refresh
11| - **User Management**: Profile management and subscription tiers
12| - **Listings**: CRUD operations for product listings
13| - **Multi-Marketplace Publishing**: Publish listings to eBay, Facebook, Craigslist
14| - **Unified Messaging**: Aggregate messages from multiple sources
15| - **AI Integration**: AI-powered descriptions, pricing, and message responses
16| - **Webhooks**: Receive notifications from email services and marketplaces
17| 
18| ## Tech Stack
19| 
20| - **FastAPI**: Modern, fast web framework
21| - **SQLAlchemy 2.0**: Async ORM for database operations
22| - **SQLite/PostgreSQL**: Database support
23| - **Alembic**: Database migrations
24| - **JWT**: Secure authentication
25| - **Pydantic**: Data validation and settings
26| - **EasyPost**: Shipping integration
27| - **Stripe**: Payment processing
28| 
29| ## Quick Start
30| 
31| ### Using Docker (Recommended)
32| 
33| 1. Clone the repository:
34| ```bash
35| cd backend
36| ```
37| 
38| 2. Copy environment file:
39| ```bash
40| cp .env.example .env
41| # Edit .env with your configuration
42| ```
43| 
44| 3. Start services:
45| ```bash
46| docker-compose up -d
47| ```
48| 
49| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
50| 
51| ### Local Development
52| 
53| 1. Install Python 3.11+
54| 
55| 2. Create virtual environment:
56| ```bash
57| python -m venv venv
58| source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
59| ```
60| 
61| 3. Install dependencies:
62| ```bash
63| pip install -r requirements.txt
64| ```
65| 
66| 4. Configure environment:
67| ```bash
68| cp .env.example .env
69| # Edit .env with your API keys (EasyPost, Stripe, etc.)
70| ```
71| 
72| 5. Initialize the database:
73| ```bash
74| python -m app.init_db
75| ```
76| 
77| 6. Run database migrations (if using PostgreSQL):
78| ```bash
79| alembic upgrade head
80| ```
81| 
82| 7. Start the server:
83| ```bash
84| uvicorn app.main:app --reload
85| ```
86| 
87| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
88| 
89| ## API Documentation
90| 
91| Once the server is running, visit:
92| - **Swagger UI**: http://localhost:8000/docs
93| - **ReDoc**: http://localhost:8000/redoc
94| 
95| ## Project Structure
96| 
97| ```
98| backend/
99| ├── app/
100| │   ├── main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
101| │   ├── config.py            # Configuration settings
102| │   ├── database.py          # Database setup
103| │   ├── models/              # SQLAlchemy models
104| │   ├── schemas/             # Pydantic schemas
105| │   ├── routers/             # API route handlers
106| │   ├── services/            # Business logic
107| │   ├── middleware/          # Authentication middleware
108| │   └── utils/               # Utility functions
109| ├── alembic/                 # Database migrations
110| ├── requirements.txt         # Python dependencies
111| ├── Dockerfile              # Container image
112| ├── docker-compose.yml      # Local dev environment
113| └── README.md               # This file
114| ```
115| 
116| ## API Endpoints
117| 
118| ### Licenses
119| - `POST /licenses/generate` - Generate license keys
120| - `GET /licenses/{key}/validate` - Validate a license key
121| - `POST /licenses/{key}/activate` - Activate a license
122| - `GET /licenses/my-licenses` - Get user's licenses
123| 
124| ### Shipping
125| - `POST /shipping/rates` - Get shipping rates
126| - `POST /shipping/labels` - Purchase a shipping label
127| - `GET /shipping/labels/{id}` - Get label details
128| - `GET /shipping/track/{tracking_number}` - Track shipment
129| - `GET /shipping/history` - Get shipment history
130| - `POST /shipping/labels/{id}/email` - Email label PDF
131| 
132| ### Pre-orders
133| - `POST /preorders/` - Create a pre-order
134| - `GET /preorders/` - List pre-orders
135| - `GET /preorders/{id}` - Get pre-order details
136| - `PATCH /preorders/{id}` - Update pre-order
137| - `POST /preorders/{id}/process-payment` - Process payment
138| 
139| ### Authentication
140| - `POST /auth/signup` - Create new user account
141| - `POST /auth/login` - Login and get tokens
142| - `POST /auth/refresh` - Refresh access token
143| 
144| ### Users
145| - `GET /users/me` - Get current user profile
146| - `PUT /users/me` - Update profile
147| - `GET /users/me/subscription` - Get subscription info
148| 
149| ### Listings
150| - `GET /listings` - List user's listings
151| - `POST /listings` - Create new listing
152| - `GET /listings/{id}` - Get listing details
153| - `PUT /listings/{id}` - Update listing
154| - `DELETE /listings/{id}` - Delete listing
155| - `POST /listings/{id}/publish` - Publish to marketplaces
156| 
157| ### Messages
158| - `GET /messages` - List messages
159| - `GET /messages/{id}` - Get message details
160| - `POST /messages/{id}/read` - Mark as read
161| - `POST /messages/{id}/reply` - Reply to message
162| - `POST /messages/{id}/ai-draft` - Generate AI response
163| 
164| ### AI
165| - `POST /ai/generate-description` - Generate product description
166| - `POST /ai/enhance-description` - Enhance description
167| - `POST /ai/suggest-price` - Get price suggestion
168| 
169| ### Marketplaces
170| - `GET /marketplaces` - List available marketplaces
171| - `POST /marketplaces/{name}/connect` - Connect marketplace
172| - `DELETE /marketplaces/{name}/disconnect` - Disconnect
173| 
174| ### Webhooks
175| - `POST /webhooks/email/inbound` - Email webhook
176| - `POST /webhooks/ebay/notification` - eBay webhook
177| - `POST /webhooks/stripe` - Stripe webhook
178| 
179| ## License Key Format
180| 
181| License keys use the format: `EMPIRE-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`
182| - 16 alphanumeric characters (excluding confusing characters like O, 0, I, 1)
183| - Always starts with "EMPIRE-"
184| - Unique and randomly generated
185| 
186| ## Database
187| 
188| Uses SQLite by default for development. For production, update `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` to use PostgreSQL:
189| ```
190| DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/empirebox
191| ```
192| 
193| ## Database Migrations
194| 
195| Create a new migration:
196| ```bash
197| alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Description of changes"
198| ```
199| 
200| Apply migrations:
201| ```bash
202| alembic upgrade head
203| ```
204| 
205| Rollback migration:
206| ```bash
207| alembic downgrade -1
208| ```
209| 
210| ## Environment Variables
211| 
212| See `.env.example` for all available configuration options.
213| 
214| Required variables:
215| - `DATABASE_URL` - Database connection string
216| - `SECRET_KEY` - Secret key for JWT signing
217| 
218| Optional variables:
219| - `EASYPOST_API_KEY` - Shipping integration
220| - `STRIPE_API_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` - Payment processing
221| - `EBAY_APP_ID`, `EBAY_CERT_ID`, `EBAY_DEV_ID` - eBay API credentials
222| - `CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY` - Email routing
223| - `GROK_API_KEY`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` - AI services
224| 
225| ## Test Mode
226| 
227| The shipping service runs in test mode by default (using simulated data). To use real EasyPost API:
228| 1. Set `EASYPOST_TEST_MODE=false` in `.env`
229| 2. Add your production `EASYPOST_API_KEY`
230| 
231| ## Testing
232| 
233| Run tests:
234| ```bash
235| pytest
236| ```
237| 
238| Run with coverage:
239| ```bash
240| pytest --cov=app tests/
241| ```
242| 
243| ## Deployment
244| 
245| ### Docker Deployment
246| 
247| 1. Build image:
248| ```bash
249| docker build -t empirebox-backend .
250| ```
251| 
252| 2. Run container:
253| ```bash
254| docker run -p 8000:8000 \
255|   -e DATABASE_URL=your_db_url \
256|   -e SECRET_KEY=your_secret_key \
257|   empirebox-backend
258| ```
259| 
260| ### Production Considerations
261| 
262| - Set `SECRET_KEY` to a secure random value
263| - Use production-grade PostgreSQL instance
264| - Enable HTTPS/TLS
265| - Set up proper CORS origins
266| - Configure rate limiting
267| - Set up logging and monitoring
268| - Use environment-specific settings
269| 
270| ## License
271| 
272| Copyright © 2026 EmpireBox. All rights reserved.
273| 
274| ## Support
275| 
276| - **Email**: support@empirebox.store
277| - **Documentation**: [docs.empirebox.store](https://docs.empirebox.store)

1| # Empire Box Agents
2| 
3| A comprehensive safeguards and emergency stop system for autonomous agents.
4| 
5| ## What Can You Do?
6| 
7| Empire Box Agents provides essential safety mechanisms for autonomous agent systems:
8| 
9| - ✅ **Emergency Stop**: Manual and automatic shutdown capabilities
10| - ✅ **Rate Limiting**: Control action frequency to prevent abuse
11| - ✅ **Budget Management**: Limit total actions to control costs
12| - ✅ **Action Whitelisting**: Restrict operations to approved actions only
13| - ✅ **State Preservation**: Save agent state during shutdowns
14| - ✅ **Admin Alerting**: Notify administrators of critical events
15| - ✅ **Thread Safety**: Secure concurrent operation handling
16| 
17| ## Quick Start
18| 
19| ```python
20| from emergency_stop import EmergencyStop
21| from safeguards import AgentSafeguards
22| 
23| # Create safeguards
24| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
25|     rate_limit=10,  # 10 actions per minute
26|     budget=100,     # 100 total actions
27|     action_whitelist=["read", "write", "analyze"]
28| )
29| 
30| # Create emergency stop
31| emergency = EmergencyStop()
32| 
33| # Execute actions safely
34| try:
35|     safeguards.can_execute_action("read")
36|     # Perform the action
37| except Exception as e:
38|     emergency.manual_trigger()
39| ```
40| 
41| ## Files
42| 
43| - **`emergency_stop.py`**: Emergency stop protocol implementation
44| - **`safeguards.py`**: Rate limiting, budgets, and action whitelisting
45| - **`integration_guide.py`**: Complete integration example and demonstration
46| - **`CAPABILITIES.md`**: Comprehensive capabilities documentation
47| 
48| ## Features in Detail
49| 
50| ### Emergency Stop System
51| 
52| Provides immediate shutdown capabilities with state preservation:
53| 
54| ```python
55| emergency = EmergencyStop()
56| 
57| # Manual trigger
58| emergency.manual_trigger()
59| 
60| # Automatic trigger based on conditions
61| emergency.auto_trigger()
62| ```
63| 
64| **Capabilities:**
65| - Manual emergency shutdown
66| - Automatic failure detection
67| - State saving before shutdown
68| - Administrator alerting
69| - Status tracking
70| 
71| ### Safeguards System
72| 
73| Controls agent behavior with multiple protection layers:
74| 
75| ```python
76| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
77|     rate_limit=5,   # Max actions per minute
78|     budget=50,      # Total action budget
79|     action_whitelist=["action1", "action2"]
80| )
81| 
82| # Check before executing action
83| safeguards.can_execute_action("action1")
84| ```
85| 
86| **Capabilities:**
87| - Rate limiting (actions per minute)
88| - Budget tracking and enforcement
89| - Action whitelisting
90| - Thread-safe operations
91| - Budget refill support
92| - Emergency budget zeroing
93| 
94| ## Integration Example
95| 
96| See `integration_guide.py` for a complete example of combining both systems:
97| 
98| ```bash
99| python integration_guide.py
100| ```
101| 
102| This demonstrates:
103| - Creating a protected agent
104| - Executing actions with safeguards
105| - Handling rate limits and budgets
106| - Triggering emergency stops
107| - Logging and monitoring
108| 
109| ## Testing
110| 
111| Run the test suite:
112| 
113| ```bash
114| python test_emergency_stop.py
115| python test_safeguards.py
116| ```
117| 
118| ## Use Cases
119| 
120| ### Development
121| - Test agent behaviors safely
122| - Experiment with different configurations
123| - Debug agent operations
124| 
125| ### Production
126| - Prevent runaway agents
127| - Control operational costs
128| - Ensure compliance with action policies
129| - Emergency intervention capabilities
130| 
131| ### High-Security
132| - Strict action controls
133| - Minimal attack surface
134| - Comprehensive audit trails
135| - Immediate shutdown on anomalies
136| 
137| ## Documentation
138| 
139| - **Quick Reference**: This README
140| - **Detailed Capabilities**: See `CAPABILITIES.md`
141| - **Integration Guide**: See `integration_guide.py`
142| - **Code Examples**: See `__main__` sections in source files
143| 
144| ## Safety Best Practices
145| 
146| 1. **Set conservative limits** initially
147| 2. **Monitor agent behavior** regularly
148| 3. **Test emergency stops** before deployment
149| 4. **Keep action whitelists minimal**
150| 5. **Review logs frequently**
151| 6. **Have manual intervention procedures**
152| 
153| ## Advanced Usage
154| 
155| ### Custom Failure Detection
156| 
157| ```python
158| class CustomEmergencyStop(EmergencyStop):
159|     def detect_failure_conditions(self):
160|         # Custom logic here
161|         if some_critical_condition():
162|             return True
163|         return False
164| ```
165| 
166| ### Dynamic Rate Limits
167| 
168| ```python
169| # Adjust rate limits at runtime
170| safeguards.rate_limit = 20  # Increase during off-peak
171| ```
172| 
173| ### Integration with Monitoring
174| 
175| ```python
176| def monitor_agent():
177|     safeguards.monitor()
178|     if safeguards.current_balance < 10:
179|         send_alert("Budget running low")
180| ```
181| 
182| ## Support
183| 
184| For detailed documentation on all capabilities, see `CAPABILITIES.md`.
185| 
186| ## License
187| 
188| See LICENSE file in the repository root.
189|

# ContractorForge API Documentation

REST API documentation for ContractorForge backend.

## Base URL

```
Development: http://localhost:8000/api/v1
Production: https://api.contractorforge.com/api/v1
```

## Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication via JWT Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
```

## Endpoints

### Health Check

#### GET /
Get API status

**Response:**
```json
{
  "name": "ContractorForge API",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "status": "online"
}
```

### Industries

#### GET /industries
List available industry templates

**Response:**
```json
{
  "industries": [
    {
      "code": "workroom",
      "name": "LuxeForge - Custom Workrooms",
      "color": "#8B4789",
      "features": {
        "production_queue": true,
        "sample_management": true
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Authentication (Coming Soon)

#### POST /auth/signup
Create new tenant and user account

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword",
  "full_name": "John Doe",
  "business_name": "John's Drapery",
  "industry_code": "workroom",
  "subscription_tier": "pro"
}
```

#### POST /auth/login
Authenticate and get access token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1Q...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "tenant_id": 1
  }
}
```

### Projects (Coming Soon)

#### GET /projects
List all projects for authenticated tenant

**Query Parameters:**
- `status` - Filter by status
- `page` - Page number
- `per_page` - Items per page

**Response:**
```json
{
  "projects": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Living Room Drapery",
      "status": "quoted",
      "customer": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Jane Smith"
      },
      "created_at": "2026-02-17T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 25,
  "page": 1,
  "pages": 3
}
```

#### POST /projects
Create new project

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "customer_id": 1,
  "name": "Kitchen Windows",
  "description": "Roman shades for 3 kitchen windows"
}
```

#### GET /projects/{id}
Get project details

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Living Room Drapery",
  "status": "quoted",
  "measurements": {
    "windows": [
      {
        "width": 48,
        "height": 72,
        "treatment_type": "drapery"
      }
    ]
  },
  "estimates": [...]
}
```

### AI Conversation (Coming Soon)

#### POST /ai/conversation/start

# ContractorForge Setup Guide

Complete installation and configuration guide for ContractorForge.

## Prerequisites

### Required Software
- **Python 3.11+**
- **Node.js 18+** and npm
- **PostgreSQL 14+**
- **Redis 6+**
- **Git**

## Backend Setup

```bash
cd contractorforge_backend
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your settings
alembic upgrade head
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

## Frontend Setup

```bash
cd contractorforge_web
npm install
echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000/api/v1" > .env.local
npm run dev
```

## External Services

1. **OpenAI API** - Get key from https://platform.openai.com/
2. **Stripe** - Get keys from https://stripe.com/
3. **SendGrid** - Get API key from https://sendgrid.com/

See full documentation in project README.

# EmpireBox Product Ecosystem

## Overview
The EmpireBox ecosystem is a comprehensive suite of business automation tools designed to help entrepreneurs build and scale their businesses. The platform consists of 23+ integrated products organized into strategic categories.

---

## Core Platform

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **EmpireBox** | Central SaaS platform — subscriptions, billing, user management, unified dashboard |
| **OpenClaw (AI Brain)** | Central AI engine powering all products — natural language, cross-product intelligence |
| **ForgeCRM** | CRM integrated across all Forge products |
| **Empire Wallet** | Custodial Solana wallet for non-crypto users |

### OpenClaw (AI Brain)
- Central AI engine powering all EmpireBox products
- Natural language conversation intake
- Cross-product intelligence and automation
- Learning system that adapts to user behavior
- **Integration**: Powers EmpireAssist, Zero to Hero automation, and all AI features

---

## Forge Products

| Product | Description | Revenue Projection Y3 |
|---------|-------------|----------------------|
| **MarketForge** | Intelligent listing creation, photo enhancement, AI descriptions | $1.9M (11%) |
| **MarketF** | P2P marketplace with escrow, Stripe Connect payments | $8.65M (50%) |
| **SupportForge** | AI-powered customer support, ticket management | Included |
| **ContractorForge** | Project/contract management for contractors | — |
| **LuxeForge** | High-end service business management | — |
| **ElectricForge** | Electrical service business template | — |
| **LandscapeForge** | Landscaping service business template | — |
| **ShipForge** | Shipping solutions, rate comparison, label generation | — |
| **LeadForge** | AI-powered lead generation (see [LEADFORGE_SPEC.md](./LEADFORGE_SPEC.md)) | — |

### MarketForge
- Intelligent listing creation tool
- Photo enhancement and optimization
- AI-powered description generation
- Multi-marketplace publishing
- Inventory management
- **Pricing**: Freemium with premium features

### MarketF (P2P Marketplace)
- Peer-to-peer marketplace platform
- Buyer and seller matching
- Integrated payments via Stripe Connect
- Rating and review system
- Dispute resolution
- **Primary Revenue Driver**

### SupportForge
- AI-powered customer support system
- Ticket management and automation
- Multi-channel support (email, chat, phone)
- Integration with EmpireAssist messaging
- Knowledge base management
- **Includes**: EmpireAssist messenger component

### ForgeCRM
**Status**: Freemium model, MVP-first approach
- Customer relationship management
- Sales pipeline tracking
- Contact management and segmentation
- Email campaigns and automation
- Integration with all Forge products

### ContractorForge
- Service business platform for contractors
- Job management and scheduling
- Quote generation and invoicing
- Photo measurements via tablet/phone
- Material tracking and ordering
- **Hardware**: Optimized for Empire Tablet

### LuxeForge
- Premium service business template
- Luxury branding and marketing
- High-end client management
- Premium pricing strategies
- White-glove customer experience

### LeadForge
- AI-powered lead generation
- Embedded in ContractorForge & LuxeForge
- Smart lead scoring and qualification
- See [LEADFORGE_SPEC.md](./LEADFORGE_SPEC.md) for full specification

---

## Revenue Generator Products

| Product | Revenue Projection Y3 | % of Total |
|---------|----------------------|------------|

"""
Configuration settings for MarketForge backend.
Loads environment variables and provides centralized config.
"""
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from typing import Optional


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Application settings loaded from environment variables."""
    
    # Database
    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost:5432/marketforge"
    
    # Security
    secret_key: str = "your-secret-key-here-change-in-production"
    algorithm: str = "HS256"
    access_token_expire_minutes: int = 30
    refresh_token_expire_days: int = 7
    
    # eBay API
    ebay_app_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_cert_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_dev_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Stripe
    stripe_secret_key: Optional[str] = None
    stripe_webhook_secret: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Cloudflare Email
    cloudflare_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    cloudflare_zone_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # AI Services
    grok_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434"
    
    # Economic Intelligence
    economic_enabled: bool = True
    economic_default_balance: float = 1000.00
    economic_token_input_price_per_1m: float = 2.50  # GPT-4 pricing per 1M tokens
    economic_token_output_price_per_1m: float = 10.00  # GPT-4 pricing per 1M tokens
    economic_compute_cost_per_minute: float = 0.10
    economic_listing_commission_rate: float = 0.05  # 5% commission on listings
    
    # Quality Evaluation
    quality_eval_enabled: bool = True
    quality_eval_model: str = "gpt-4-turbo-preview"
    
    # CORS
    cors_origins: list = ["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8080"]
    
    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"
        case_sensitive = False


# Global settings instance
settings = Settings()

# ContractorForge Deployment Guide

Production deployment instructions for ContractorForge.

## Deployment Options

1. **Docker Compose** (Recommended for self-hosting)
2. **Cloud Platforms** (Railway, Render, Heroku)
3. **Vercel + Supabase** (For Next.js frontend)
4. **AWS/GCP/Azure** (Enterprise)

## Option 1: Docker Compose (Recommended)

### Prerequisites
- Docker 20+
- Docker Compose 2+
- Domain name with DNS configured

### 1. Create docker-compose.yml

```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:14-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: contractorforge
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: contractorforge
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
      - contractorforge

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    networks:
      - contractorforge

  backend:
    build: ./contractorforge_backend
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+asyncpg://contractorforge:${DB_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/contractorforge
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${STRIPE_SECRET_KEY}
      SECRET_KEY: ${SECRET_KEY}
      ENVIRONMENT: production
    depends_on:
      - postgres
      - redis
    networks:
      - contractorforge
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"

  frontend:
    build: ./contractorforge_web
    environment:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: https://api.yourdomain.com/api/v1
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    networks:
      - contractorforge

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf
      - ./ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl
    depends_on:
      - backend
      - frontend
    networks:
      - contractorforge

volumes:
  postgres_data:

networks:
  contractorforge:
```

### 2. Create .env File

```bash
DB_PASSWORD=your_secure_password
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-key
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_your-key
SECRET_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
```

### 3. Deploy

```bash
# Build and start
docker-compose up -d

# View logs
docker-compose logs -f

# Run migrations
docker-compose exec backend alembic upgrade head

# Scale services
docker-compose up -d --scale backend=3
```

## Option 2: Cloud Platforms

### Railway (Recommended)

#### Backend Deployment

1. Go to https://railway.app/
2. Click "New Project" > "Deploy from GitHub repo"
3. Select your repository
4. Set root directory to `contractorforge_backend`
5. Add environment variables:
   ```
   DATABASE_URL (auto-provided by PostgreSQL plugin)
   REDIS_URL (auto-provided by Redis plugin)
   OPENAI_API_KEY
   STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
   SECRET_KEY
   ```
6. Add PostgreSQL and Redis plugins
7. Deploy!

#### Frontend Deployment

1. New service in same project
2. Set root directory to `contractorforge_web`

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Build application
RUN npm run build

# Production stage
FROM node:18-alpine AS runner

WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Copy necessary files
COPY --from=builder /app/next.config.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder /app/.next ./.next
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json

# Create non-root user
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs
RUN adduser -S nextjs -u 1001
RUN chown -R nextjs:nodejs /app
USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["npm", "start"]

"""
AI routes for content generation and assistance.
"""
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List, Optional
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from app.models import User
from app.middleware import get_current_user
from app.services.ai_service import AIService
from app.database import get_db

router = APIRouter(prefix="/ai", tags=["AI"])


class GenerateDescriptionRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request to generate product description."""
    title: str
    category: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    photos: List[str] = []


class EnhanceDescriptionRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request to enhance existing description."""
    current_description: str


class SuggestPriceRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request to suggest pricing."""
    title: str
    category: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    description: Optional[str] = None


@router.post("/generate-description")
async def generate_description(
    request: GenerateDescriptionRequest,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
):
    """
    Generate product description using AI.
    """
    ai_service = AIService(current_user, db)
    
    result = await ai_service.generate_description({
        "title": request.title,
        "category": request.category,
        "condition": request.condition,
        "photos": request.photos
    })
    
    return result


@router.post("/enhance-description")
async def enhance_description(
    request: EnhanceDescriptionRequest,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
):
    """
    Enhance existing product description.
    """
    ai_service = AIService(current_user, db)
    
    result = await ai_service.enhance_description(request.current_description)
    
    return result


@router.post("/suggest-price")
async def suggest_price(
    request: SuggestPriceRequest,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
):
    """
    Suggest pricing for a product.
    """
    ai_service = AIService(current_user, db)
    
    result = await ai_service.suggest_price({
        "title": request.title,
        "category": request.category,
        "condition": request.condition,
        "description": request.description
    })
    
    return result

# Telegram Bot Setup Guide

Control and monitor your EmpireBox remotely via Telegram.

## Prerequisites

- EmpireBox installed and running
- A Telegram account

---

## Step 1 — Create a Telegram Bot

1. Open Telegram and search for **@BotFather**
2. Send `/newbot` and follow the prompts
3. Copy the **bot token** you receive (looks like `123456:ABC-...`)

---

## Step 2 — Get Your Telegram User ID

1. Open Telegram and search for **@userinfobot**
2. Send `/start` — it will reply with your numeric user ID (e.g. `123456789`)

---

## Step 3 — Configure the Bot

Edit `/opt/empirebox/telegram/config.yaml`:

```yaml
telegram:
  bot_token: "123456:ABC-your-actual-token"

  allowed_users:
    - 123456789   # Replace with your Telegram user ID

  pin_enabled: false   # Set to true to require a PIN for destructive commands
  pin_code: "1234"

  pin_required_commands:
    - stop_product
    - stop_all
    - restart

  rate_limit:
    max_commands_per_minute: 30

  notifications:
    enabled: true
    system_alerts: true
    product_crashes: true
    daily_summary: false
    daily_summary_time: "09:00"

openclaw:
  host: "http://openclaw:7878"
  timeout: 30
```

---

## Step 4 — Add Bot Token to the Environment

Edit `/opt/empirebox/.env` and set:

```
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456:ABC-your-actual-token
```

---

## Step 5 — Start the Bot

```bash
ebox telegram start
```

Check status:

```bash
ebox telegram status
```

View logs:

```bash
ebox telegram logs
```

Stop the bot:

```bash
ebox telegram stop
```

---

## Step 6 — Test the Bot

Open Telegram, find your bot by the username you gave it in Step 1, and send:

```
/start
```

You should receive a welcome message.

---

## Available Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `/start` | Welcome message and setup info |
| `/status` | Show running products |
| `/list` | List all products with status |
| `/start_product <name>` | Start a product (e.g. `/start_product marketforge`) |
| `/stop_product <name>` | Stop a product |
| `/bundle <name>` | Start a bundle (`reseller`, `contractor`, `support`, `full`) |
| `/logs <product>` | Get recent logs for a product |
| `/health` | Full system health check |
| `/backup` | Trigger database backup |
| `/help` | Show all commands |

You can also send **natural language** messages:

- "What's running right now?"
- "Start MarketForge"
- "How much disk space is left?"
- "Show me the last errors"

The bot forwards these to the OpenClaw AI agent and returns the response.

---

## Security

### Whitelist

Only users listed in `allowed_users` can interact with the bot. All other messages are silently rejected.

### PIN Protection

1| # EmpireBox Backend API
2| 
3| FastAPI backend for EmpireBox - combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, and AI-powered marketplace automation.
4| 
5| ## Features
6| 
7| - **License Key System**: Generate, validate, and activate license keys for hardware bundles
8| - **Shipping Integration**: EasyPost integration for comparing rates and purchasing labels
9| - **Pre-order Management**: Handle hardware bundle pre-orders with Stripe payments
10| - **Authentication**: JWT-based auth with signup, login, and token refresh
11| - **User Management**: Profile management and subscription tiers
12| - **Listings**: CRUD operations for product listings
13| - **Multi-Marketplace Publishing**: Publish listings to eBay, Facebook, Craigslist
14| - **Unified Messaging**: Aggregate messages from multiple sources
15| - **AI Integration**: AI-powered descriptions, pricing, and message responses
16| - **Webhooks**: Receive notifications from email services and marketplaces
17| 
18| ## Tech Stack
19| 
20| - **FastAPI**: Modern, fast web framework
21| - **SQLAlchemy 2.0**: Async ORM for database operations
22| - **SQLite/PostgreSQL**: Database support
23| - **Alembic**: Database migrations
24| - **JWT**: Secure authentication
25| - **Pydantic**: Data validation and settings
26| - **EasyPost**: Shipping integration
27| - **Stripe**: Payment processing
28| 
29| ## Quick Start
30| 
31| ### Using Docker (Recommended)
32| 
33| 1. Clone the repository:
34| ```bash
35| cd backend
36| ```
37| 
38| 2. Copy environment file:
39| ```bash
40| cp .env.example .env
41| # Edit .env with your configuration
42| ```
43| 
44| 3. Start services:
45| ```bash
46| docker-compose up -d
47| ```
48| 
49| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
50| 
51| ### Local Development
52| 
53| 1. Install Python 3.11+
54| 
55| 2. Create virtual environment:
56| ```bash
57| python -m venv venv
58| source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
59| ```
60| 
61| 3. Install dependencies:
62| ```bash
63| pip install -r requirements.txt
64| ```
65| 
66| 4. Configure environment:
67| ```bash
68| cp .env.example .env
69| # Edit .env with your API keys (EasyPost, Stripe, etc.)
70| ```
71| 
72| 5. Initialize the database:
73| ```bash
74| python -m app.init_db
75| ```
76| 
77| 6. Run database migrations (if using PostgreSQL):
78| ```bash
79| alembic upgrade head
80| ```
81| 
82| 7. Start the server:
83| ```bash
84| uvicorn app.main:app --reload
85| ```
86| 
87| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
88| 
89| ## API Documentation
90| 
91| Once the server is running, visit:
92| - **Swagger UI**: http://localhost:8000/docs
93| - **ReDoc**: http://localhost:8000/redoc
94| 
95| ## Project Structure
96| 
97| ```
98| backend/
99| ├── app/
100| │   ├── main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
101| │   ├── config.py            # Configuration settings
102| │   ├── database.py          # Database setup
103| │   ├── models/              # SQLAlchemy models
104| │   ├── schemas/             # Pydantic schemas
105| │   ├── routers/             # API route handlers
106| │   ├── services/            # Business logic
107| │   ├── middleware/          # Authentication middleware
108| │   └── utils/               # Utility functions
109| ├── alembic/                 # Database migrations
110| ├── requirements.txt         # Python dependencies
111| ├── Dockerfile              # Container image
112| ├── docker-compose.yml      # Local dev environment
113| └── README.md               # This file
114| ```
115| 
116| ## API Endpoints
117| 
118| ### Licenses
119| - `POST /licenses/generate` - Generate license keys
120| - `GET /licenses/{key}/validate` - Validate a license key
121| - `POST /licenses/{key}/activate` - Activate a license
122| - `GET /licenses/my-licenses` - Get user's licenses
123| 
124| ### Shipping
125| - `POST /shipping/rates` - Get shipping rates
126| - `POST /shipping/labels` - Purchase a shipping label
127| - `GET /shipping/labels/{id}` - Get label details
128| - `GET /shipping/track/{tracking_number}` - Track shipment
129| - `GET /shipping/history` - Get shipment history
130| - `POST /shipping/labels/{id}/email` - Email label PDF
131| 
132| ### Pre-orders
133| - `POST /preorders/` - Create a pre-order
134| - `GET /preorders/` - List pre-orders
135| - `GET /preorders/{id}` - Get pre-order details
136| - `PATCH /preorders/{id}` - Update pre-order
137| - `POST /preorders/{id}/process-payment` - Process payment
138| 
139| ### Authentication
140| - `POST /auth/signup` - Create new user account
141| - `POST /auth/login` - Login and get tokens
142| - `POST /auth/refresh` - Refresh access token
143| 
144| ### Users
145| - `GET /users/me` - Get current user profile
146| - `PUT /users/me` - Update profile
147| - `GET /users/me/subscription` - Get subscription info
148| 
149| ### Listings
150| - `GET /listings` - List user's listings
151| - `POST /listings` - Create new listing
152| - `GET /listings/{id}` - Get listing details
153| - `PUT /listings/{id}` - Update listing
154| - `DELETE /listings/{id}` - Delete listing
155| - `POST /listings/{id}/publish` - Publish to marketplaces
156| 
157| ### Messages
158| - `GET /messages` - List messages
159| - `GET /messages/{id}` - Get message details
160| - `POST /messages/{id}/read` - Mark as read
161| - `POST /messages/{id}/reply` - Reply to message
162| - `POST /messages/{id}/ai-draft` - Generate AI response
163| 
164| ### AI
165| - `POST /ai/generate-description` - Generate product description
166| - `POST /ai/enhance-description` - Enhance description
167| - `POST /ai/suggest-price` - Get price suggestion
168| 
169| ### Marketplaces
170| - `GET /marketplaces` - List available marketplaces
171| - `POST /marketplaces/{name}/connect` - Connect marketplace
172| - `DELETE /marketplaces/{name}/disconnect` - Disconnect
173| 
174| ### Webhooks
175| - `POST /webhooks/email/inbound` - Email webhook
176| - `POST /webhooks/ebay/notification` - eBay webhook
177| - `POST /webhooks/stripe` - Stripe webhook
178| 
179| ## License Key Format
180| 
181| License keys use the format: `EMPIRE-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`
182| - 16 alphanumeric characters (excluding confusing characters like O, 0, I, 1)
183| - Always starts with "EMPIRE-"
184| - Unique and randomly generated
185| 
186| ## Database
187| 
188| Uses SQLite by default for development. For production, update `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` to use PostgreSQL:
189| ```
190| DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/empirebox
191| ```
192| 
193| ## Database Migrations
194| 
195| Create a new migration:
196| ```bash
197| alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Description of changes"
198| ```
199| 
200| Apply migrations:
201| ```bash
202| alembic upgrade head
203| ```
204| 
205| Rollback migration:
206| ```bash
207| alembic downgrade -1
208| ```
209| 
210| ## Environment Variables
211| 
212| See `.env.example` for all available configuration options.
213| 
214| Required variables:
215| - `DATABASE_URL` - Database connection string
216| - `SECRET_KEY` - Secret key for JWT signing
217| 
218| Optional variables:
219| - `EASYPOST_API_KEY` - Shipping integration
220| - `STRIPE_API_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` - Payment processing
221| - `EBAY_APP_ID`, `EBAY_CERT_ID`, `EBAY_DEV_ID` - eBay API credentials
222| - `CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY` - Email routing
223| - `GROK_API_KEY`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` - AI services
224| 
225| ## Test Mode
226| 
227| The shipping service runs in test mode by default (using simulated data). To use real EasyPost API:
228| 1. Set `EASYPOST_TEST_MODE=false` in `.env`
229| 2. Add your production `EASYPOST_API_KEY`
230| 
231| ## Testing
232| 
233| Run tests:
234| ```bash
235| pytest
236| ```
237| 
238| Run with coverage:
239| ```bash
240| pytest --cov=app tests/
241| ```
242| 
243| ## Deployment
244| 
245| ### Docker Deployment
246| 
247| 1. Build image:
248| ```bash
249| docker build -t empirebox-backend .
250| ```
251| 
252| 2. Run container:
253| ```bash
254| docker run -p 8000:8000 \
255|   -e DATABASE_URL=your_db_url \
256|   -e SECRET_KEY=your_secret_key \
257|   empirebox-backend
258| ```
259| 
260| ### Production Considerations
261| 
262| - Set `SECRET_KEY` to a secure random value
263| - Use production-grade PostgreSQL instance
264| - Enable HTTPS/TLS
265| - Set up proper CORS origins
266| - Configure rate limiting
267| - Set up logging and monitoring
268| - Use environment-specific settings
269| 
270| ## License
271| 
272| Copyright © 2026 EmpireBox. All rights reserved.
273| 
274| ## Support
275| 
276| - **Email**: support@empirebox.store
277| - **Documentation**: [docs.empirebox.store](https://docs.empirebox.store)

1| # Empire Box Agents
2| 
3| A comprehensive safeguards and emergency stop system for autonomous agents.
4| 
5| ## What Can You Do?
6| 
7| Empire Box Agents provides essential safety mechanisms for autonomous agent systems:
8| 
9| - ✅ **Emergency Stop**: Manual and automatic shutdown capabilities
10| - ✅ **Rate Limiting**: Control action frequency to prevent abuse
11| - ✅ **Budget Management**: Limit total actions to control costs
12| - ✅ **Action Whitelisting**: Restrict operations to approved actions only
13| - ✅ **State Preservation**: Save agent state during shutdowns
14| - ✅ **Admin Alerting**: Notify administrators of critical events
15| - ✅ **Thread Safety**: Secure concurrent operation handling
16| 
17| ## Quick Start
18| 
19| ```python
20| from emergency_stop import EmergencyStop
21| from safeguards import AgentSafeguards
22| 
23| # Create safeguards
24| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
25|     rate_limit=10,  # 10 actions per minute
26|     budget=100,     # 100 total actions
27|     action_whitelist=["read", "write", "analyze"]
28| )
29| 
30| # Create emergency stop
31| emergency = EmergencyStop()
32| 
33| # Execute actions safely
34| try:
35|     safeguards.can_execute_action("read")
36|     # Perform the action
37| except Exception as e:
38|     emergency.manual_trigger()
39| ```
40| 
41| ## Files
42| 
43| - **`emergency_stop.py`**: Emergency stop protocol implementation
44| - **`safeguards.py`**: Rate limiting, budgets, and action whitelisting
45| - **`integration_guide.py`**: Complete integration example and demonstration
46| - **`CAPABILITIES.md`**: Comprehensive capabilities documentation
47| 
48| ## Features in Detail
49| 
50| ### Emergency Stop System
51| 
52| Provides immediate shutdown capabilities with state preservation:
53| 
54| ```python
55| emergency = EmergencyStop()
56| 
57| # Manual trigger
58| emergency.manual_trigger()
59| 
60| # Automatic trigger based on conditions
61| emergency.auto_trigger()
62| ```
63| 
64| **Capabilities:**
65| - Manual emergency shutdown
66| - Automatic failure detection
67| - State saving before shutdown
68| - Administrator alerting
69| - Status tracking
70| 
71| ### Safeguards System
72| 
73| Controls agent behavior with multiple protection layers:
74| 
75| ```python
76| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
77|     rate_limit=5,   # Max actions per minute
78|     budget=50,      # Total action budget
79|     action_whitelist=["action1", "action2"]
80| )
81| 
82| # Check before executing action
83| safeguards.can_execute_action("action1")
84| ```
85| 
86| **Capabilities:**
87| - Rate limiting (actions per minute)
88| - Budget tracking and enforcement
89| - Action whitelisting
90| - Thread-safe operations
91| - Budget refill support
92| - Emergency budget zeroing
93| 
94| ## Integration Example
95| 
96| See `integration_guide.py` for a complete example of combining both systems:
97| 
98| ```bash
99| python integration_guide.py
100| ```
101| 
102| This demonstrates:
103| - Creating a protected agent
104| - Executing actions with safeguards
105| - Handling rate limits and budgets
106| - Triggering emergency stops
107| - Logging and monitoring
108| 
109| ## Testing
110| 
111| Run the test suite:
112| 
113| ```bash
114| python test_emergency_stop.py
115| python test_safeguards.py
116| ```
117| 
118| ## Use Cases
119| 
120| ### Development
121| - Test agent behaviors safely
122| - Experiment with different configurations
123| - Debug agent operations
124| 
125| ### Production
126| - Prevent runaway agents
127| - Control operational costs
128| - Ensure compliance with action policies
129| - Emergency intervention capabilities
130| 
131| ### High-Security
132| - Strict action controls
133| - Minimal attack surface
134| - Comprehensive audit trails
135| - Immediate shutdown on anomalies
136| 
137| ## Documentation
138| 
139| - **Quick Reference**: This README
140| - **Detailed Capabilities**: See `CAPABILITIES.md`
141| - **Integration Guide**: See `integration_guide.py`
142| - **Code Examples**: See `__main__` sections in source files
143| 
144| ## Safety Best Practices
145| 
146| 1. **Set conservative limits** initially
147| 2. **Monitor agent behavior** regularly
148| 3. **Test emergency stops** before deployment
149| 4. **Keep action whitelists minimal**
150| 5. **Review logs frequently**
151| 6. **Have manual intervention procedures**
152| 
153| ## Advanced Usage
154| 
155| ### Custom Failure Detection
156| 
157| ```python
158| class CustomEmergencyStop(EmergencyStop):
159|     def detect_failure_conditions(self):
160|         # Custom logic here
161|         if some_critical_condition():
162|             return True
163|         return False
164| ```
165| 
166| ### Dynamic Rate Limits
167| 
168| ```python
169| # Adjust rate limits at runtime
170| safeguards.rate_limit = 20  # Increase during off-peak
171| ```
172| 
173| ### Integration with Monitoring
174| 
175| ```python
176| def monitor_agent():
177|     safeguards.monitor()
178|     if safeguards.current_balance < 10:
179|         send_alert("Budget running low")
180| ```
181| 
182| ## Support
183| 
184| For detailed documentation on all capabilities, see `CAPABILITIES.md`.
185| 
186| ## License
187| 
188| See LICENSE file in the repository root.
189|

# ContractorForge API Documentation

REST API documentation for ContractorForge backend.

## Base URL

```
Development: http://localhost:8000/api/v1
Production: https://api.contractorforge.com/api/v1
```

## Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication via JWT Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
```

## Endpoints

### Health Check

#### GET /
Get API status

**Response:**
```json
{
  "name": "ContractorForge API",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "status": "online"
}
```

### Industries

#### GET /industries
List available industry templates

**Response:**
```json
{
  "industries": [
    {
      "code": "workroom",
      "name": "LuxeForge - Custom Workrooms",
      "color": "#8B4789",
      "features": {
        "production_queue": true,
        "sample_management": true
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Authentication (Coming Soon)

#### POST /auth/signup
Create new tenant and user account

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword",
  "full_name": "John Doe",
  "business_name": "John's Drapery",
  "industry_code": "workroom",
  "subscription_tier": "pro"
}
```

#### POST /auth/login
Authenticate and get access token

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1Q...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "tenant_id": 1
  }
}
```

### Projects (Coming Soon)

#### GET /projects
List all projects for authenticated tenant

**Query Parameters:**
- `status` - Filter by status
- `page` - Page number
- `per_page` - Items per page

**Response:**
```json
{
  "projects": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Living Room Drapery",
      "status": "quoted",
      "customer": {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Jane Smith"
      },
      "created_at": "2026-02-17T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 25,
  "page": 1,
  "pages": 3
}
```

#### POST /projects
Create new project

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "customer_id": 1,
  "name": "Kitchen Windows",
  "description": "Roman shades for 3 kitchen windows"
}
```

#### GET /projects/{id}
Get project details

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Living Room Drapery",
  "status": "quoted",
  "measurements": {
    "windows": [
      {
        "width": 48,
        "height": 72,
        "treatment_type": "drapery"
      }
    ]
  },
  "estimates": [...]
}
```

### AI Conversation (Coming Soon)

#### POST /ai/conversation/start

# ContractorForge Setup Guide

Complete installation and configuration guide for ContractorForge.

## Prerequisites

### Required Software
- **Python 3.11+**
- **Node.js 18+** and npm
- **PostgreSQL 14+**
- **Redis 6+**
- **Git**

## Backend Setup

```bash
cd contractorforge_backend
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your settings
alembic upgrade head
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

## Frontend Setup

```bash
cd contractorforge_web
npm install
echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000/api/v1" > .env.local
npm run dev
```

## External Services

1. **OpenAI API** - Get key from https://platform.openai.com/
2. **Stripe** - Get keys from https://stripe.com/
3. **SendGrid** - Get API key from https://sendgrid.com/

See full documentation in project README.

# EmpireBox Product Ecosystem

## Overview
The EmpireBox ecosystem is a comprehensive suite of business automation tools designed to help entrepreneurs build and scale their businesses. The platform consists of 23+ integrated products organized into strategic categories.

---

## Core Platform

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **EmpireBox** | Central SaaS platform — subscriptions, billing, user management, unified dashboard |
| **OpenClaw (AI Brain)** | Central AI engine powering all products — natural language, cross-product intelligence |
| **ForgeCRM** | CRM integrated across all Forge products |
| **Empire Wallet** | Custodial Solana wallet for non-crypto users |

### OpenClaw (AI Brain)
- Central AI engine powering all EmpireBox products
- Natural language conversation intake
- Cross-product intelligence and automation
- Learning system that adapts to user behavior
- **Integration**: Powers EmpireAssist, Zero to Hero automation, and all AI features

---

## Forge Products

| Product | Description | Revenue Projection Y3 |
|---------|-------------|----------------------|
| **MarketForge** | Intelligent listing creation, photo enhancement, AI descriptions | $1.9M (11%) |
| **MarketF** | P2P marketplace with escrow, Stripe Connect payments | $8.65M (50%) |
| **SupportForge** | AI-powered customer support, ticket management | Included |
| **ContractorForge** | Project/contract management for contractors | — |
| **LuxeForge** | High-end service business management | — |
| **ElectricForge** | Electrical service business template | — |
| **LandscapeForge** | Landscaping service business template | — |
| **ShipForge** | Shipping solutions, rate comparison, label generation | — |
| **LeadForge** | AI-powered lead generation (see [LEADFORGE_SPEC.md](./LEADFORGE_SPEC.md)) | — |

### MarketForge
- Intelligent listing creation tool
- Photo enhancement and optimization
- AI-powered description generation
- Multi-marketplace publishing
- Inventory management
- **Pricing**: Freemium with premium features

### MarketF (P2P Marketplace)
- Peer-to-peer marketplace platform
- Buyer and seller matching
- Integrated payments via Stripe Connect
- Rating and review system
- Dispute resolution
- **Primary Revenue Driver**

### SupportForge
- AI-powered customer support system
- Ticket management and automation
- Multi-channel support (email, chat, phone)
- Integration with EmpireAssist messaging
- Knowledge base management
- **Includes**: EmpireAssist messenger component

### ForgeCRM
**Status**: Freemium model, MVP-first approach
- Customer relationship management
- Sales pipeline tracking
- Contact management and segmentation
- Email campaigns and automation
- Integration with all Forge products

### ContractorForge
- Service business platform for contractors
- Job management and scheduling
- Quote generation and invoicing
- Photo measurements via tablet/phone
- Material tracking and ordering
- **Hardware**: Optimized for Empire Tablet

### LuxeForge
- Premium service business template
- Luxury branding and marketing
- High-end client management
- Premium pricing strategies
- White-glove customer experience

### LeadForge
- AI-powered lead generation
- Embedded in ContractorForge & LuxeForge
- Smart lead scoring and qualification
- See [LEADFORGE_SPEC.md](./LEADFORGE_SPEC.md) for full specification

---

## Revenue Generator Products

| Product | Revenue Projection Y3 | % of Total |
|---------|----------------------|------------|

"""
Configuration settings for MarketForge backend.
Loads environment variables and provides centralized config.
"""
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from typing import Optional


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Application settings loaded from environment variables."""
    
    # Database
    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost:5432/marketforge"
    
    # Security
    secret_key: str = "your-secret-key-here-change-in-production"
    algorithm: str = "HS256"
    access_token_expire_minutes: int = 30
    refresh_token_expire_days: int = 7
    
    # eBay API
    ebay_app_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_cert_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_dev_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Stripe
    stripe_secret_key: Optional[str] = None
    stripe_webhook_secret: Optional[str] = None

    cloudflare_zone_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # AI Services

    ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434"
    
    # Economic Intelligence
    economic_enabled: bool = True
    economic_default_balance: float = 1000.00
    economic_token_input_price_per_1m: float = 2.50  # GPT-4 pricing per 1M tokens
    economic_token_output_price_per_1m: float = 10.00  # GPT-4 pricing per 1M tokens
    economic_compute_cost_per_minute: float = 0.10
    economic_listing_commission_rate: float = 0.05  # 5% commission on listings
    
    # Quality Evaluation
    quality_eval_enabled: bool = True
    quality_eval_model: str = "gpt-4-turbo-preview"
    
    # CORS
    cors_origins: list = ["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8080"]
    
    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"
        case_sensitive = False
